basic.title: Basic Information
basic.description: Start by defining the unit title and duration
basic.guidance: Select the subject and class for this unit. In IB MYP, unit titles are typically framed as engaging questions that provoke inquiry and exploration (e.g., 'How does energy shape our world?'). Assign a unit number and specify the duration (weeks and hours per week).

inquiry.title: Inquiry Question
inquiry.description: Define the central question that drives student inquiry
inquiry.guidance: The inquiry question should be open-ended, thought-provoking, and aligned with the Statement of Inquiry. It guides student exploration throughout the unit.

concepts.title: Key & Related Concepts
concepts.description: Identify the conceptual understanding for this unit
concepts.guidance: Key Concepts are 16 broad, transferable ideas in IB MYP (select 1-3). Related Concepts are subject-specific and vary by discipline (enter as comma-separated). Global Context connects learning to real-world issues that transcend subject boundaries.

statement.title: Statement of Inquiry
statement.description: Create the statement that synthesizes concepts and context
statement.guidance: The Statement of Inquiry integrates Key Concept + Related Concept + Global Context into a clear statement that guides the entire unit.

attributes.title: Learner Profile & Service
attributes.description: Define IB learner attributes and service learning opportunities
attributes.guidance: Select IB Learner Profile attributes (e.g., Inquirers, Thinkers, Communicators) students will develop. Identify opportunities for service learning and action.

learning.title: Learning Process
learning.description: Outline how students will learn and engage
learning.guidance: Describe the learning experiences, activities, and teaching strategies. Include differentiation strategies and how students will construct understanding.

assessment.title: Assessment & Relationship
assessment.guidance: Formative assessments monitor learning progress. Summative assessments evaluate achievement. Explain how the summative assessment relates back to the Statement of Inquiry.

fields.subject: Subject
fields.assessmentRelationship: Relationship: Summative Assessment ↔ Statement of Inquiry
fields.noClassesAvailable: No classes available for this subject

weeklyPlanTab.reflectionPlaceholder: How did this week go? What worked well? What needs adjustment?

weeklyPlanTab.noData: No weekly plan data available for this topic.
weeklyPlanTab.noDataHint: The system will auto-generate weeks based on topic duration.
weeklyPlanTab.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all weekly plans for "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

report.title: Student Progress Report
report.subtitle: Generate and preview student progress reports
report.classInfoBanner: {count} students found in this class. All reports will be generated into 1 ZIP file.
report.infoText: Select academic year, semester, class, and student then click "Preview Report" to view and print the student progress report.

mentorCommentTab.title: Mentor Comment
mentorCommentTab.subtitle: Write semester homeroom comments for each student in your class.
mentorCommentTab.selectPrompt: Select class and semester to start writing mentor comments
mentorCommentTab.noStudents: No students found in this class
mentorCommentTab.commentPlaceholder: Write your mentor comment for this student...
mentorCommentTab.aiHelpTooltip: Generate a new comment from scratch. Fill in a few bullet points and AI will write the report card comment.
mentorCommentTab.refineTooltip: Improve an existing comment. AI will simplify the language and remove any over-the-top phrases.
mentorCommentTab.downloadTemplateTooltip: Download a blank Excel file pre-filled with student names. Fill in the comment column then import it back.
mentorCommentTab.importExcelTooltip: Upload the filled Excel template to load all comments at once. Make sure to use the downloaded template format.

tour.step1Desc: This tab lets you write semester report card comments for each student. Start by filling in the 3 filters below.
tour.step2Desc: Pick the subject you want to write comments for. Available subjects are based on your teaching assignments.
tour.step3Desc: After selecting a subject, choose the class. Available classes will load automatically.
tour.step4Desc: Choose Semester 1 or 2. The student list with comment boxes will appear below.
tour.step5Desc: Once all 3 filters are set, each student appears with a text box. Type directly in the box. There is a character counter to help you stay within the limit.
tour.step6Desc: Click the purple AI Help button next to any student. Enter 2 or 3 bullet points about that student and AI will write a full report card comment for you.
tour.step7Desc: Already have a comment typed? Click the orange Refine button and AI will improve the language. The result will be simpler, more professional, and free of over-the-top phrases.
tour.step8Desc: Need to fill many comments at once? Click Download Template to get a pre-filled Excel file. Fill in the comment column, then click Import Excel to load everything at once.
tour.step9Desc: Click Save on each student to save individually. Or use Save All in the toolbar to save all comments at once. Unsaved comments are highlighted in yellow.
